pi_items.source: Downloaded from https://wiki.wireshark.org/uploads/__moin_import__/attachments/SampleCaptures/dns.cap and verified with file command and tshark
Tested with tshark -Y filter on dns.cap; confirmed to isolate DNS query packets (response flag = 0)
Verified in task.json description field and export_result.sh script
Executed: tshark -r dns.cap -Y 'dns.flags.response == 0' | wc -l; Result: 19 DNS query packets
Executed: tshark -r dns.cap | wc -l; Total packets in dns.cap is 38 (19 queries + 19 responses)

privileged_info_summary: The dns.cap file from official Wireshark sample captures contains 38 total DNS packets: 19 DNS query packets (dns.flags.response == 0) and 19 DNS response packets (dns.flags.response == 1). The metadata correctly specifies the pcap filename as 'dns.cap', the display filter as 'dns.flags.response == 0' to isolate DNS queries, and the output filename as 'dns_query_count.txt'. The expected ground truth answer is 19 DNS queries. All data comes from real official Wireshark sample captures, not synthetic data.
